assumptions: Please act as a professional verilog designer.

Implement a module to achieve serial input data accumulation output, input is 8bit data. The valid_in will be set to 1 before the first data comes in. Whenever the module receives 4 input data, the data_out outputs 4 received data accumulation results and sets the valid_out to be 1 (will last only 1 cycle).
data_out[9:0]: 10-bit output data representing the accumulated sum.
When valid_in is 1, data_in is a valid input. Accumulate four valid input data_in values and calculate the output data_out by adding these four values together.
There is no output when there are fewer than four data_in inputs in the interim. Along with the output data_out, a cycle of valid_out=1 will appear as a signal.
The valid_out signal is set to 1 when the data_out outputs 4 received data accumulation results. Otherwise, it is set to 0.
Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.
LLM planning note: All added cases respect the contract's signal set; no new ports or protocols are introduced.
LLM planning note: Timing assumptions remain conservative, avoiding fixed‑latency claims due to the contract's unknown latency model.

expected_properties: Observed outputs or protocol-visible signals settle to a stable, non-X post-reset state.
No fixed-cycle completion is assumed during reset recovery.
cases.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
notes: Reset polarity may still be heuristic if the contract marks it ambiguous.
preconditions: Reset signal is available in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: A legal operation causes an observable state or output change after conservative observation.
The plan does not assume a completion event before it is externally visible.
When four consecutive cycles present valid_in=1 with arbitrary data_in values, a single-cycle valid_out pulse appears and data_out equals the arithmetic sum of the four inputs.
No valid_out pulse occurs before four valid inputs have been received.
cases.goal: Verify correct accumulation of four valid inputs and single‑cycle valid_out indication under conservative timing.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
The exact cycle offset of valid_out relative to the fourth input is not assumed; observation window must be sufficient to capture the pulse.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known inputs: ['clk', 'rst_n', 'data_in', 'valid_in']
Drive a sequence of four data_in values (e.g., 8'h01, 8'h02, 8'h03, 8'h04) with valid_in asserted for each cycle.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Observe the design for at least 8 clock cycles after the first valid input to allow the accumulator to produce the valid_out pulse.

expected_properties: Boundary-value stimulation does not rely on hidden state or undocumented latency.
When width expressions are symbolic, only conservative min/max-style exploration is planned.
cases.goal: Exercise boundary-value and width-sensitive input patterns.
notes: Edge coverage remains value-oriented and avoids fixed-latency assumptions.
preconditions: At least one input or symbolic-width input is available.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['data_in']
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: Repeated operations do not rely on hidden fixed-latency assumptions.
The second operation is observed only through externally visible acceptance, completion, or output change.
cases.goal: Observe repeated or back-to-back legal operations under conservative timing assumptions.
notes: When timing is unresolved, this case stays unresolved-safe and does not require deterministic overlap behavior.
preconditions: At least one legal operation is already defined by the basic case.
stimulus_intent: Apply two legal operations with minimal idle spacing that remains safe for the current contract strength.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: When valid_in is 0, changes on data_in must not affect internal accumulator.
valid_out must remain 0 regardless of data_in activity while valid_in is low.
cases.goal: Confirm that the design correctly ignores data_in when valid_in is deasserted and does not produce spurious valid_out pulses.
notes: This case checks that no hidden side‑effects occur for illegal input conditions.
stimulus_intent: Hold valid_in low for several cycles while toggling data_in with random values.
timing_assumptions: Observe over a window of at least 8 clock cycles after deasserting reset.

expected_properties: Accumulating four maximum 8‑bit values (255) yields a 10‑bit sum of 1020.
valid_out is asserted for exactly one cycle after the fourth valid input.
cases.goal: Validate correct handling of the maximum possible sum and single‑cycle valid_out pulse under conservative timing.
notes: Ensures no overflow beyond the declared 10‑bit width.
preconditions: Design is out of reset and valid_in is asserted.
stimulus_intent: Drive data_in with 8'hFF for four consecutive cycles while valid_in=1.
timing_assumptions: Monitor for at least 6 cycles after the first valid input to capture the valid_out pulse.

unresolved_items: Detected valid_ready-like signal(s) ['valid_in'] without matching role(s) ['ready'] in group 'in'.
Detected valid_ready-like signal(s) ['valid_out'] without matching role(s) ['ready'] in group 'out'.
valid_ready handshake ambiguity remains; tests avoid assuming a ready signal.
